result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/3/patron-flesh/writs-page-width.md`.

Key calls I made:

- **Reframed the ask as alignment, not redesign.** The brief implies "apply some margins and maybe cards" — I anchored it on "Writs conforms to the Spider/Guild page-chrome convention" (#26). Writs is the non-conforming one; the existing pages are the authority.
- **Named a specific reader and decision** (#22) — patron/operator scanning mandate state in Oculus during sessions, multiple times a day.
- **Scope held tight to chrome only** (#23) — explicitly excluded columns, filters, interactions, empty states, and any touch-ups to Spider/Guild.
- **Flagged the upstream-vs-downstream fork** (#31) — if Spider and Guild share a layout component, wrap Writs in it; if they each define their own layout, extract the shared chrome so the next new page can't drift.
- **Pushed back on defining a new card primitive** (#8) — card treatment should pull from the existing Oculus idiom; if there isn't one, I want to weigh in before a net-new visual primitive is introduced as a side effect.
- **Deferred questions the planner should answer before dispatch** — whether a shared layout exists, whether a card component exists, what sibling widgets live on the page, and whether other pages also drift.
